citation.description: check_verify_gate_parity.py implements a parity drift-guard that fails the build when vote.needs in gerrit-verify.yaml is not a superset of every unconditional gating job across the push/PR mirror lanes

criteria.criterion: There is a guard to ensure that new CI checks are included in the gerrit verify job (preventing drift that causes failures on main after merge)

citation.description: The 'Verified-gate parity gate' step runs python scripts/check_verify_gate_parity.py inside the reusable _build-and-test.yml, shared by both test.yml and gerrit-verify.yaml

criteria.criterion: The parity guard runs in CI (on every push/PR and gerrit verify)

citation.description: vote.needs now lists: [clear-vote, require-ticket, build-and-test, optionality, artifact-probe, eval-discipline, golden-path, verify-identity]

criteria.criterion: The specific jobs that previously caused green-verify/red-main (artifact-probe, eval-discipline) are now included in vote.needs

citation.description: _artifact-probe.yml and _eval-discipline.yml are reusable workflow_call workflows invoked by both test.yml and gerrit-verify.yaml, documented as fixing jira-reb-1163

criteria.criterion: artifact-probe and eval-discipline are implemented as reusable workflows shared between test.yml and gerrit-verify.yaml (no drift by construction)

citation.description: test_real_repo_verify_gate_is_in_parity() and test_real_repo_check_main_returns_zero() assert the committed workflows are in parity

criteria.criterion: Tests exist to verify the parity guard works and that the real repo is in parity
